metadata.title: Configure PostgreSQL Database with Flyway Migrations

story.iWant: configure PostgreSQL database schema with Flyway migrations for ITSM and PM modules
story.soThat: we can persist domain data with proper structure, indexes, and CQRS-lite read models for high-performance queries

tasks.task: Configure Flyway in Gradle build (6 subtasks)
Create ITSM OLTP migration V1 (14 subtasks)
Create PM OLTP migration V2 (12 subtasks)
Create shared tables migration V3 (14 subtasks)
Create CQRS-lite read models migration V4 (10 subtasks)
Add integration test with Testcontainers (11 subtasks)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Flyway dependencies are configured in build.gradle.kts and migrations execute automatically on application startup
Migration V1__create_itsm_tables.sql creates ITSM OLTP tables: tickets, incidents, service_requests, ticket_comments, routing_rules with UUIDv7 primary keys and appropriate indexes
Migration V2__create_pm_tables.sql creates PM OLTP tables: issues, sprints, boards, board_columns, issue_links with UUIDv7 primary keys and appropriate indexes
Migration V3__create_shared_tables.sql creates shared tables: users, roles, user_roles, teams, approvals, workflow_states, audit_log, and outbox (transactional event log)
Migration V4__create_read_models.sql creates CQRS-lite read models: ticket_card, sla_tracking, related_incidents, queue_row, issue_card, sprint_summary with denormalized fields for <200ms p95 queries
All migrations execute successfully against local Docker Compose PostgreSQL 16+ instance
Integration test with Testcontainers validates schema creation, table existence, and basic CRUD operations

doc.title: Epic 1 Foundation Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Complete SQL schema definitions for all 28 tables: 5 ITSM OLTP (tickets, incidents, service_requests, ticket_comments, routing_rules) + 5 PM OLTP (issues, sprints, boards, board_columns, issue_links) + 8 Shared (users, roles, user_roles, teams, approvals, workflow_states, audit_log, outbox) + 6 Read Models (ticket_card, sla_tracking, related_incidents, queue_row, issue_card, sprint_summary). Includes UUIDv7 implementation guide, optimistic locking patterns, transactional outbox pattern, and CQRS-lite read model update strategy.

doc.title: Epic 1 Foundation Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Hibernate 7.0+ @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7) usage for time-ordered, globally unique primary keys. Example Ticket entity with @Id, @Version for optimistic locking, and FK relationships. UUIDv7 provides 6.6x better B-tree index performance vs UUIDv4 due to time-ordered structure per RFC 9562.

doc.title: Epic 1 Foundation Technical Specification
doc.snippet: PostgreSQL 16+ sizing: 4 vCPU, 16GB RAM, 500GB SSD. High Availability via streaming replication (active-passive). HikariCP connection pool with maximumPoolSize=50. Schema strategy: OLTP tables (normalized source of truth) + Read Models (denormalized for hot reads, updated by Event Worker).

doc.title: Architecture Blueprint - SynergyFlow
doc.snippet: Modulith + Companions deployment pattern. Backend App (HTTP/SSE), Event Worker (outbox drainer), Timer/SLA Service (durable scheduler), Search Indexer (OpenSearch). PostgreSQL 16 as primary datastore, Redis Streams for event fan-out, Keycloak for authentication.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: PostgreSQL 16+ primary datastore. OLTP tables (normalized) for domain writes. Read models (denormalized) for hot reads. Flyway for versioned schema migrations. Continuous WAL archiving to S3. RPO: 15 minutes, RTO: 4 hours.

doc.title: Story 1.1: Initialize Monorepo Structure
doc.snippet: Docker Compose PostgreSQL service defined in infrastructure/docker-compose.yml with synergyflow database, postgres:16 image, port 5432.

doc.title: Story 1.2: Set up Spring Boot Modulith Architecture
doc.snippet: 8 modules established: itsm, pm, workflow, security, eventing, sla, sse, audit. Each has api/, spi/, internal/, events/ subdirectories. Spring Modulith 1.2.4 with build-time verification tests (ModularityTests.java, ArchUnitTests.java). JPA entities will go in module internal/domain/ packages.

artifact.reason: Current dependencies include spring-boot-starter-data-jpa (line 22) and postgresql driver (line 24). Story 1.3 must add org.flywaydb:flyway-core and org.flywaydb:flyway-database-postgresql dependencies here.
Testcontainers dependencies needed for AC7 integration test. Add org.testcontainers:postgresql and org.testcontainers:testcontainers-bom to testImplementation.
Main Spring Boot entry point. When application starts, Flyway will auto-run migrations if spring.flyway.enabled=true in application.yml.
ITSM module where JPA entities (Ticket, Incident, ServiceRequest, TicketComment) will be placed in internal/domain/ subdirectory in Story 1.4+.
PM module where JPA entities (Issue, Sprint, Board, BoardColumn, IssueLink) will be placed in internal/domain/ subdirectory in Story 1.4+.
PostgreSQL 16 container for local development. Database: synergyflow, User: synergyflow, Password: synergyflow, Port: 5432. Used for AC6 manual migration testing.

backend.dependency: REQUIRED: org.flywaydb:flyway-core (missing - add in this story)
REQUIRED: org.flywaydb:flyway-database-postgresql (missing - add in this story)
REQUIRED: org.testcontainers:postgresql (missing - add in this story for AC7)
REQUIRED: org.testcontainers:junit-jupiter (missing - add in this story for AC7)

constraints.constraint: Spring Boot Modulith pattern enforced via @ApplicationModule annotations. Database migrations belong in shared infrastructure, NOT in individual modules. Place migrations in backend/src/main/resources/db/migration/ (global resource directory).

All primary keys MUST use UUID type with application-generated UUIDv7 via Hibernate @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7). Database columns defined as UUID, NOT VARCHAR(36). Application generates IDs to ensure same ID is used in domain logic and outbox events.

Flyway migrations are immutable once applied to production. Never modify existing V1, V2, V3, V4 migration files. New schema changes require new migration files (V5, V6, etc.). Migration naming convention: V{version}__{description}.sql (double underscore).

All OLTP tables MUST include version BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 column for Hibernate optimistic locking. Prevents lost updates in concurrent scenarios. Application increments version on each update; database rejects if version mismatch.

Transactional outbox pattern requires outbox table with id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY for FIFO ordering, processed_at TIMESTAMP for at-least-once delivery, and UNIQUE constraint on (aggregate_id, version) for idempotency.

CQRS-lite read models (V4 migration) are denormalized copies updated by Event Worker. Include version BIGINT column for idempotent updates. Event Worker drops stale events by comparing version numbers.

Critical indexes required: idx_tickets_status, idx_issues_status, idx_outbox_unprocessed (partial WHERE processed_at IS NULL), idx_sla_due_at (partial WHERE NOT paused), idx_ticket_card_sla_due (with NULLS LAST). Indexes drive query performance targets: queue loads <200ms p95, side-panel hydration <300ms p95.

Integration tests MUST use Testcontainers with real PostgreSQL 16 container, NOT H2 or in-memory mocks. Ensures schema compatibility with production database. Tests validate Flyway execution, table existence, and index creation.
Flyway configuration in application.yml: spring.flyway.enabled=true, spring.flyway.locations=classpath:db/migration. Default baseline-on-migrate=false to prevent accidental production baselining.

interface.name: Flyway API
interface.signature: org.flywaydb.core.Flyway - Spring Boot auto-configuration via spring.flyway.* properties
interface.usage: Migrations auto-execute on Spring Boot startup when spring.flyway.enabled=true. Flyway scans classpath:db/migration for V*.sql files, tracks applied migrations in flyway_schema_history table.

interface.name: Hibernate UUIDv7 Generator
interface.usage: Generates time-ordered, globally unique UUIDs per RFC 9562. Place on @Id UUID fields in JPA entities. Hibernate inserts generated UUID before persist, making ID available immediately after save() call.

interface.name: JPA @Version
interface.usage: Enables optimistic locking. Hibernate auto-increments version on each update. If version mismatch (concurrent update), throws OptimisticLockException. Maps to version BIGINT NOT NULL columns in migrations.

interface.name: Testcontainers PostgreSQL
interface.usage: Spins up real PostgreSQL Docker container for integration tests. Use @DynamicPropertySource to override spring.datasource.url with container URL. Container auto-stops after tests complete.

interface.name: JdbcTemplate
interface.usage: Execute raw SQL queries in integration tests to validate table existence, index creation, and Flyway migration success. Example: jdbcTemplate.queryForObject("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM flyway_schema_history", Integer.class)

tests.standards: Integration tests use Testcontainers with real PostgreSQL 16 container to validate Flyway migrations. Tests verify: (1) flyway_schema_history table shows 4 successful migrations, (2) All 28 tables exist via information_schema.tables query, (3) Critical indexes exist via pg_indexes query. Test class annotated with @SpringBootTest and @Testcontainers. Uses @DynamicPropertySource to inject container datasource URL. JUnit 5 (Jupiter) framework with standard Spring Boot Test dependency. No business logic tests yet - Story 1.3 validates schema only. JPA entity and repository tests deferred to Story 1.4+.

locations.location: backend/src/test/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/ModularityTests.java - existing from Story 1.2, no changes needed
backend/src/test/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/ArchUnitTests.java - existing from Story 1.2, no changes needed

ideas.idea: Test: Verify Flyway auto-configuration by checking Spring Boot logs for "Migrating schema" message during application startup
Test: testFlywayMigrationsExecuted() - Query flyway_schema_history table and assert 4 rows with success=true
Test: testItsmTablesCreated() - Query information_schema.tables for tickets, incidents, service_requests, ticket_comments, routing_rules and assert all exist
Test: testPmTablesCreated() - Query information_schema.tables for issues, sprints, boards, board_columns, issue_links and assert all exist
Test: testSharedTablesCreated() - Query information_schema.tables for users, roles, user_roles, teams, approvals, workflow_states, audit_log, outbox and assert all exist
Test: testReadModelsCreated() - Query information_schema.tables for ticket_card, sla_tracking, related_incidents, queue_row, issue_card, sprint_summary and assert all exist
Manual test: docker-compose up -d postgres && ./gradlew bootRun && psql -h localhost -U synergyflow -d synergyflow -c "\\dt" | wc -l should show 28+ lines (28 tables + header)
Test: testIndexesCreated() - Query pg_indexes WHERE indexname IN ('idx_tickets_status', 'idx_outbox_unprocessed', 'idx_sla_due_at', 'idx_ticket_card_sla_due') and assert COUNT(*)=4+
Test: testOutboxUniqueConstraint() - Attempt to insert duplicate (aggregate_id, version) and assert SQLException with "unique constraint" message
Test: testTicketsVersionColumn() - Insert ticket row, update it, query version column and assert value=2 (incremented from default 1)
